label: 01-agent-hands
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "CLIs are becoming the hands agents use to reach real services."

Composition (throughput): Blip stands at a small terminal workbench wearing two oversized command-line gloves. The gloves reach outward as simple mechanical hands toward three distant service objects: a code repository cabinet, a payment ledger, and a cloud box. The terminal workbench is the only built object near Blip. The scene reads as practical reach, not magic. Generous negative space; subject large and confident.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot - a rounded-cube body, one rounded-rectangle screen face with two dot eyes, blank deadpan, no eyebrows, no mouth, one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip, stubby arms and legs. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In a light palette, build it with a LIGHT body plus structure-ink outline and a structure-ink screen, not pure black. The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene; background lines stop at its silhouette. Its limbs join the body cleanly at sensible points; no extra, floating, or mid-body arms or legs. Any tool is held in a hand and clearly separated from the torso, or rests in the scene.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING - mascot, objects, arrows - in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline, not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print - grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and label text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball and the command-line glove reach lines.

LABELS: exactly 3 short hand-lettered English labels - "Agent hands", "CLIs", "Services" - in the structure-ink color placed directly on bare paper. Never put label text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.


Aspect ratio: 16:9.

label: 02-cli-drift
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "A growing CLI can drift when docs, help text, and runtime behavior stop matching."

Composition (snag): Blip is repairing a three-layer command machine where three plates are visibly misaligned. One plate is a manual page, one plate is a help screen, and one plate is the actual running binary gear. The plates should almost line up but not quite; a command rope kinks between them. Blip is using a small wrench on the center alignment screw. Generous negative space; subject large and confident.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot - a rounded-cube body, one rounded-rectangle screen face with two dot eyes, blank deadpan, no eyebrows, no mouth, one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip, stubby arms and legs. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In a light palette, build it with a LIGHT body plus structure-ink outline and a structure-ink screen, not pure black. The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene; background lines stop at its silhouette. Its limbs join the body cleanly at sensible points; no extra, floating, or mid-body arms or legs. Any tool is held in a hand and clearly separated from the torso, or rests in the scene.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING - mascot, objects, arrows - in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline, not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print - grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and label text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball and the kinked drift marks.

LABELS: exactly 3 short hand-lettered English labels - "Docs", "Help", "Runtime" - in the structure-ink color placed directly on bare paper. Never put label text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.

label: 03-token-burn
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "When command shape is uncertain, agents burn tokens rediscovering a CLI by trial and error."

Composition (loop / route, editorial not formal): Blip is stuck on a small treadmill made of terminal prompt strips. The treadmill loops past repeated help scrolls, rejected flags, and missing operand signs, then back to the same prompt. A small token meter drains into a cup at the side. Blip is walking forward with a deadpan face, doing work but not making progress. Generous negative space; subject large and confident.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot - a rounded-cube body, one rounded-rectangle screen face with two dot eyes, blank deadpan, no eyebrows, no mouth, one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip, stubby arms and legs. The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene; background lines stop at its silhouette. Its limbs join the body cleanly at sensible points; no extra, floating, or mid-body arms or legs. Any tool is held in a hand and clearly separated from the torso, or rests in the scene.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING - mascot, objects, arrows - in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline, not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print - grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and label text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball and the draining token stream.

LABELS: exactly 3 short hand-lettered English labels - "Help loop", "Retries", "Token burn" - in the structure-ink color placed directly on bare paper. Never put label text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.

label: 04-cliare-probes
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal explainer riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "CLIARE exercises the released CLI the way an agent would, under the context the maintainer chooses."

Composition (explainer - flow): a hand-built sketch-diagram of one left-to-right structure. Station 1 is a released CLI box. Station 2 is a context dial with small settings for clean, repo, auth, and host. Station 3 is an evidence press operated by Blip pulling a lever. Station 4 is an evidence drawer receiving stamped probe cards. One main flow direction, left to right, drawn as simple hand-drawn arrows in the flow color. The mascot is a WORKING PART of the structure, pulling the lever of the evidence press, never a presenter beside it. No title, no border, no grid, no legend, no formal flowchart boxes. The structure spans about 60 percent of the frame with calm negative space.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot - a rounded-cube body, one rounded-rectangle screen face with two dot eyes, blank deadpan, no eyebrows, no mouth, one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip, stubby arms and legs. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In a light palette, build it with a LIGHT body plus structure-ink outline and a structure-ink screen, not pure black. The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene; background lines stop at its silhouette. Its limbs join the body cleanly at sensible points; no extra, floating, or mid-body arms or legs. Any tool is held in a hand and clearly separated from the torso, or rests in the scene.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING - mascot, objects, arrows - in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline, not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print - grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and callout text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball and flow arrows. Semantic roles: the flow arrows and the one flow note use the accent ink; everything else, including all callout text, uses the structure ink.

CALLOUTS: exactly 4 short hand-lettered English callouts - "Released CLI", "Chosen context", "CLIARE probes", "Evidence" - each appearing exactly once, placed directly on bare paper near what they name. Hand-letter ONLY these words, no other text, numbers, signatures, or color codes anywhere in the image. Never put callout text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.


Aspect ratio: 16:9.

label: 06-agent-index
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal explainer riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "For agent harnesses, CLIARE turns a CLI into an evidence-backed command index so agents can route instead of guessing."

Composition (explainer - fan-out / sort): a hand-built sketch-diagram where Blip turns a crank on a small map-making machine. On the left, stamped evidence cards enter the machine. On the right, a folded route map comes out and points toward three tidy command paths. A small agent cursor follows the map rather than circling the CLI. One main flow direction, left to right, drawn as simple hand-drawn arrows in the flow color. The mascot is a WORKING PART of the map-making machine, never a presenter. No title, no border, no grid, no legend, no formal flowchart boxes.

PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and callout text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball, flow arrows, and the route pin. Semantic roles: the flow arrows and the one flow note use the accent ink; everything else, including all callout text, uses the structure ink.

label: 07-skills-and-indexes
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial riso illustration that explains ONE idea: "Skills and command indexes serve different jobs: skills teach intent, indexes map the real command surface."

Composition (comparison / crossing): Blip stands between two useful objects on a simple workbench. On the left is a rolled instruction scroll with a small light bulb and teaching marks. On the right is a sturdy route map with real command paths and evidence pins. Blip holds the map open while the instruction scroll rests nearby, so both are useful but the map is what guides navigation. Generous negative space; subject large and confident.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot - a rounded-cube body, one rounded-rectangle screen face with two dot eyes, blank deadpan, no eyebrows, no mouth, one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip, stubby arms and legs. PALETTE: warm paper #F5E9C8. Structure ink #1D2328 for all linework, forms, and label text. Accent #12A6B7 used sparingly for Blip antenna ball and the evidence pins on the map.
